Container.Name: factorio-tools-manager
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: factorio game server multiplayer dedicated srv mods modpack
Container.Overview:
Run and manage **many Factorio dedicated servers** from one web UI.
This container is the manager only. It creates each Factorio server as its own sibling container through the Docker socket, so servers keep running across a manager update and are managed entirely from the UI: create, start/stop, live RCON console and container logs, saves and backups, mods and shared modpacks, map-generation settings with per-planet previews, whitelists, and per-server Factorio version.
Optionally, each server can be reachable at its own subdomain with no port number to type (for example factory1.yourdomain.com), using Cloudflare DNS SRV records plus a built-in DDNS job. That is configured in the UI, not here, and the app works fine without it.
[b]Before you start[/b] [ol] [li]Pick a contiguous UDP port range for your games (default 34197-34297) and forward it on your router to this server, using the SAME external and internal ports. The manager only ever hands out ports from that range, so every server it creates is reachable.[/li] [li]Set ADMIN_PASSWORD below. It is the only required setting.[/li] [/ol]
[b]Notes[/b] [ul] [li]Game ports are published by the individual server containers, not by this one, so there are no game ports to map here.[/li] [li]The Factorio servers it creates appear in the Docker tab without their own templates. That is expected — manage them from this app's UI.[/li] [li]RCON is never exposed off the host and never appears in DNS.[/li] [li]The Space Age mods ship with the game and require the DLC; they are enabled, never downloaded.[/li] [/ul]
Container.Requires: A contiguous UDP port range forwarded on your router (default 34197-34297), and access to the Docker socket so the manager can create the server containers. Cloudflare DNS is optional and configured inside the app.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the web UI. Put a TLS reverse proxy in front if you expose it beyond your LAN.

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Everything persistent lives here: the manager database and each server's saves, mods and config. The manager detects the host path behind this mount automatically, so the Factorio containers it creates bind-mount the right directory.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Required. The manager creates each Factorio server as a sibling container through this socket. Treat access to the web UI as equivalent to host access.

Config.Name: Admin Password
Config.Description: Required. The password for the web UI login.

Config.Name: Game Port Range
Config.Description: The contiguous UDP range you forwarded on your router. Servers are only ever given ports from inside it, so this must match your port forward.

Config.Name: RCON Port Range
Config.Description: TCP range for server admin (RCON). Published on host loopback only — never forward this.

Config.Name: Data Dir
Config.Description: Where the data lives inside the container. Must match the container side of the Appdata mount above — change both or neither.

Config.Name: Session Secret
Config.Description: Signs the login session cookie. Leave blank to derive one, or set your own (openssl rand -hex 32). Changing it logs everyone out.

Config.Name: Factorio Image
Config.Description: Base image for the game servers. Each server picks its own tag (stable / latest / a specific version) in the UI; this sets the repository.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID the Factorio containers run as, which owns the files under appdata. 845 matches the Factorio image default; set 99 for the usual Unraid 'nobody'.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID the Factorio containers run as. 845 matches the Factorio image default; set 100 for the usual Unraid 'users'.

Config.Name: Resume Servers On Startup
Config.Description: Restart the servers that were running when the manager last stopped.

Config.Name: Stop Servers On Shutdown
Config.Description: Stop every Factorio server when the manager stops. Off by default so games survive a manager update.
